threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think Kingsland Road is a shortcut instead of a traffic jam waiting to happen. You'll learn soon enough!
Caught the 196 last night; a guy was trying to argue with a seagull over chips. The bus driver just sighed and kept driving.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I heard a toddler arguing with their grandma in Turkish over ice cream flavors at the corner shop. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you find a kebab place that doubles as an art gallery, complete with edgy installations and late-night DJ sets. Truly unique!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a true local when they know to avoid the 'doughnut' intersection during rush hour—it's a tangle of taxis and tourists.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy trying to train his parrot on the 19. It squawked every time the bus stopped. Definitely added to the commute chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all locals drink Cava at 10 AM, but the real morning drink is a cortado, preferably with a pastry from the nearest fleca.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with the T10 card machine at the metro, as if it’s a piece of alien technology.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You keep looking for bus stops instead of finding the nearest 'stop' for Uber and Bolt. That's a dead giveaway you’re new to Dalston.
Heard a girl on the 333 explaining her tattoo choices to a sleep-deprived bloke. Turns out, they were both heading to the same late shift in Vauxhall.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The first time I saw a guy jogging with his dog, while carrying a reusable coffee cup and a vinyl record under his arm. Classic Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do they host an underground rave in the U-Bahn tunnels, complete with glow sticks and an impromptu light show. Just wild.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local when they know to walk through the alley behind La Taqueria to avoid the 6th Street crowd during lunch.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full gorilla suit trying to board the 5-Fulton at Civic Center. No one batted an eye. Just another Tuesday in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists flock to the Gaudí spots but miss the hidden murals in the alleys—Gràcia's art tells stories that often go unheard.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still think the transport is reliable, waiting at the metro during the strikes like it’s just a bad day. Good luck with that!
